title: Create and Write to a File
description:
## Create a File With Specific Contents

Using the terminal below, create a file and write a single word into it.

**Your tasks:**
1. Create a file at **`/root/hello.txt`**
2. Its contents must include the word **`kubekosh`**

> 💡 You can do both in one step with output redirection (`>`), or create the file first and edit it. When you're done, open the **Validate** tab and run the checks.

hints.title: Write text to a file in one command
hints.body: The `>` operator redirects a command's output into a file, creating it if it doesn't exist (and overwriting it if it does).

hints.title: Verify the contents
hints.body: Use cat to print the file and confirm the word is there before validating.

validation.description: Checks that /root/hello.txt exists and contains the word 'kubekosh'.
